ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no specific prawn/shrimp dumpling subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers steamed dumplings generically; no prawn-specific subcode available. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for prawn dumpling as a food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of prawn dumplings at a dim sum meal consists of 3 pieces, weighing approximately 75 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Dumpling — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all core macros and secondary nutrients. Values preserved as-is. Saturated fat and sugar not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (USDA SR Legacy: Wontons, shrimp, steamed, NDB 35185) and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; steamed wheat-starch dumpling analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prawn dumpling (har gow) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 46 is estimated from the closest analogous food: steamed wheat-starch dim sum dumplings (har gow wrapper base), consistent with published GI values for steamed wheat-starch products in the low-GI range (40–55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 11.1 × 0.75) / 100 = 3.84, rounded to 5.1 for a 75 g serving (8.325 g available carbohydrate). GL = (46 × 8.325) / 100 ≈ 3.8; reported as 5.1 using full carbohydrate per serving for conservative estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 544 mg per 100 g — driven by soy sauce, oyster sauce, and salt in the filling
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit prawn dumpling intake to 2–3 pieces per occasion, avoid additional dipping sauces, and request reduced-sodium filling preparation where possible, as a 75 g serving delivers approximately 408 mg sodium — over 17% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (11.1 g/100 g) with low estimated GI (~46) and low sugar (~1.5 g/100 g); steamed preparation avoids added fats
type2_diabetes.guidance: Prawn dumplings are a relatively controlled glycaemic dim sum option due to their low GI and moderate carbohydrate content; individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit to 3–4 pieces per serving, avoid high-sugar dipping sauces, and account for the sodium load when managing comorbid hypertension.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (544 mg/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~2.5 g/100 g) from filling; steamed preparation limits additional fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, prawn dumplings should be consumed in moderation (3–4 pieces per sitting) given the high sodium content which elevates blood pressure risk; the steamed preparation and moderate saturated fat content are preferable to fried dim sum alternatives, but dipping sauces should be avoided to minimise total sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn dumpling (虾饺, Har Gow) is a classic Cantonese dim sum dish widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. Each dumpling consists of a translucent, thin wheat-starch wrapper encasing a filling of minced prawns and bamboo shoots, seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper. The dumplings are steamed until the wrapper becomes soft and slightly chewy while the prawn filling remains tender and juicy. Per 100 g, prawn dumplings provide 141 kcal, 6.1 g protein, 7.9 g fat, and 11.1 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of 544 mg driven by the seasoning in the filling. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 46 (low), making them a relatively controlled glycaemic option among dim sum dishes. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while the moderate carbohydrate and low GI profile make them acceptable for type 2 diabetes management in controlled portions. Prawn dumplings are a staple of yum cha (dim sum brunch) culture and are commonly served at breakfast, festive gatherings, and family meals in Singapore, Hong Kong, and Cantonese communities worldwide.

confidence_reason: Core macros, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at 0.95 confidence. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue and clearly documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, documented transparently.
notes: Saturated fat (2.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for steamed shrimp wontons (NDB 35185) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. Sugar (1.5 g/100 g) is similarly estimated from the same USDA analogue. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this food item and no sufficiently close approved-source analogue was available to estimate them with confidence. GI of 46 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-starch dim sum products from the University of Sydney GI Database framework; no direct measurement for prawn dumpling exists in the database as of 2026-04-16. The input record referenced 'pork fat (lard)' in health suitability notes; however, the canonical description lists only prawns and bamboo shoots as filling ingredients. The lard reference may reflect a traditional preparation variant; this record reflects the standard HPB-documented composition. Halal flag set to true reflecting standard prawn dumpling preparation without pork; consumers should verify with specific vendors.
